task_completion.description: Open the countries_base project or load the ne_110m_populated_places layer in gvSIG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gvSIG application is open, and the 'ne_110m_populated_places' layer is visible in the Table of Contents on the left or displayed on the map view.

task_completion.description: Measure the distance between New York and London.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gvSIG measurement tool is active, showing a line drawn between the approximate locations of New York (North America) and London (Europe), with a distance value displayed in the measurement dialog.

task_completion.description: Measure the distance between Tokyo and Sydney.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gvSIG measurement tool is active, showing a line drawn between the approximate locations of Tokyo (Japan) and Sydney (Australia), with a distance value displayed.

task_completion.description: Measure the distance between Buenos Aires and Cape Town.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gvSIG measurement tool is active, showing a line drawn between the approximate locations of Buenos Aires (South America) and Cape Town (South Africa), with a distance value displayed.

task_completion.description: Create the distances.txt file at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) or terminal command (e.g., echo, cat) showing the creation or editing of a file named 'distances.txt' in the '~/gvsig_data/exports/' directory.

task_completion.description: Record the measured distances in the correct format within the acceptable 15% tolerance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of distances.txt show exactly three lines: 'New York-London: <value> km', 'Tokyo-Sydney: <value> km', and 'Buenos Aires-Cape Town: <value> km'. The values must fall within these ranges: NY-London (4747-6422), Tokyo-Sydney (6623-8960), and BA-Cape Town (5856-7923).

integrity.description: The agent used the gvSIG GUI to measure the distances.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent interacting with the gvSIG map and measurement tools, rather than using a Python script or terminal utility (like pyproj or ogr2ogr) to calculate the distances programmatically.

integrity.description: The agent actually measured the distances on the map rather than looking them up online or hardcoding them.
integrity.visual_evidence: At least one screenshot should show the measurement line physically drawn between the correct continents/cities in the gvSIG map view.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely created the output file in the environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file creation must be visible in the terminal or a text editor, proving the agent didn't just output the final text into the chat without saving it to the requested path.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/distances.txt'. The true WGS84 geodesic distances are approximately: New York-London = 5585 km, Tokyo-Sydney = 7792 km, Buenos Aires-Cape Town = 6890 km. The task allows a 15% tolerance. Acceptable ranges are: NY-London (4747 - 6422 km), Tokyo-Sydney (6623 - 8960 km), BA-Cape Town (5856 - 7923 km). The file format must be exactly: '[City1]-[City2]: <value> km'.
